an2.135:1.1: “Endowed with two qualities, the fool—incompetent, a person of no integrity—keeps himself injured & impaired. He’s blameworthy, open to being blamed by the observant, & generates much demerit.
an2.135:1.3: “Without investigating it, without examining it, he shows confidence in a matter that merits skepticism.
an2.135:1.4: Without investigating it, without examining it, he shows skepticism toward a matter that merits confidence.
an2.135:1.5: “Endowed with these two qualities, the fool—incompetent, a person of no integrity—keeps himself injured & impaired. He’s blameworthy, open to being blamed by the observant, & generates much demerit.
an2.135:2.1: “Endowed with two qualities, the wise person—competent, a person of integrity—keeps himself uninjured & unimpaired. He’s blameless, not open to being blamed by the observant, & generates much merit.
an2.135:2.3: “Investigating it, examining it, he shows skepticism toward a matter that merits skepticism.
an2.135:2.4: Investigating it, examining it, he shows confidence in a matter that merits confidence.
an2.135:2.5: “Endowed with these two qualities, the wise person—competent, a person of integrity—keeps himself uninjured & unimpaired. He’s blameless, not open to being blamed by the observant, & generates much merit.”
